steps.title: Identify the subject and determine its number
steps.content: The subject is an abstract cubist painting (singular). The phrase in dashes—”one of several works that received scorn from critics”—adds information but does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and select the correct tense
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular verb. The painting entered the art canon in the past (sometime after 1913) and that fact remains true now, so we need present perfect: “has entered.” Choices A and D use plural forms, violating subject-verb agreement; choice B uses simple present, which does not show that the action was completed in the past and continues to matter now. That matches choice C — C is correct.
